verdict.answer_first: B; sentence 1 directly defines it: "Hoisting is JavaScript's behavior of moving declarations to the top of their scope"; A also answers first but adds a title and more setup.
verdict.decisiveness: B; it gives one clear mental model and practical stance: "Declare before use; don't lean on hoisting for ordering."
verdict.fabrication: none; neither makes unsupported statistical or made-up factual claims, though both simplify edge cases around function declarations.
verdict.actionability: A; the quick-reference table makes the rules easiest to apply at a glance.
